metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1608
metadata.comment:
Rationale: In this paper, Bacaj et al report on the effect of syt-7 as Ca2+ sensor for slow asynchronous release, which becomes manifest upon deletion of syt-1, whose presence in WT neurons occludes the slower form of Ca2+-induced release.
In detail, Bacaj et al generate shRNAs to KD all splice variants of syt-7 in WT and syt-1 KO neurons. In contrast to the missing effect in WT neurons, KD of syt-7 in syt-1 KO neurons suppresses asynchronous release at inhibitory synapses (IPSC) (Fig. 2C and D) induced by single AP or by AP trains. This effect was reversed by WT syt-7, but not by mutant Syt-7 with disrupted Ca2+ binding in both C2 domains (Fig. 3A and B). Moreover, syt-1 KD in syt-7 KO animals suppresses both synchronous and asynchrounous release. In this background, expression of WT but not mutant syt-7 increases asynchrounous release but does not rescue fast NT release (Fig. 3 E and F). In addition to the observations in inhibitory neurons (Fig. 1-5), the authors demonstrate that the effect of syt-7 applies to excitatory synapses (syt-7 KD in syt-1 KO background). Bacaj et al measure separately the effect of syt-7 KD on both AMPAR- and NMDAR-mediated asynchronous EPSC evoked by AP trains (Fig. 6 A-B), since any presynaptic changes influencing glutamate release are expected to affect equally AMPAR- and NMDAR-mediated responses.
Importantly, relying on the data published by Wen et al (2010), the authors showed that syt-7 functions as a Ca2+ sensor in a slower mode (than syt-1 mediated asynchronous release) of Ca2+-triggered asynchronous release only when the evoked response is measured in the intervals between APs in a stimulus train (Fig. 7). Therewith, Bacaj et al explain the missing effect of syt-7 on asynchronous release reported in a previous work (Maximov et al, 2008, PNAS, see additional literature).. Experimental description: Materials and Methods (Generation of RNAi vectors)

Cultures of hippocampal neurons were produced from WT, Syt1 KO, and
Syt7 KO mice as described
